headline: New data at AACR 2026 demonstrate advancements in Cancerguard® Multi-Cancer Early Detection Test
summary: Abbott (NYSE: ABT) will present new data at the American Association for Cancer Research (AACR) Annual Meeting 2026 demonstrating continued advancements in its multi-biomarker, multi-cancer early detection (MCED) program supporting the commercially available Cancerguard® test. Additionally, the AACR Cancer Prevention Research Award for Outstanding Journal Article will recognize a publication on MCED multiyear outcomes from the DETECT-A study.

summary: The China Diabetes Devices Market is projected to grow from USD 7.19 billion in 2025 to USD 14.51 billion by 2034, marking a CAGR of 8.11% from 2026 to 2034. headline: US Stock Market Today S&P 500 Futures Edge Higher As Inflation Jitters Linger
summary: The Morning Bull - US Market Morning Update Friday, Apr, 17 2026 US stock futures are pointing slightly higher this morning, with E-mini S&P 500 contracts up about 0.2%, as investors weigh firm bond yields against mixed economic signals. The 10 year US Treasury yield is holding above 4.3% while oil prices stay elevated, which together keep borrowing costs and inflation worries in focus for households and rate sensitive sectors such as real estate and utilities. headline: TSMC's Chip Heat Extends the Record Run, Then Netflix Drops a Double Surprise After the Bell
summary: Thursday was the second consecutive record close for the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq, and for most of the session the driver was straightforward: TSMC's quarterly numbers were the kind of print that leaves the semiconductor bulls feeling vindicated. Then Netflix reported after the bell, and within minutes the stock was down 8% on two pieces of news the market clearly hadn't priced in, a softer forward outlook and the departure of Reed Hastings from the chairman's seat.

raw.headline: TSMC's Chip Heat Extends the Record Run, Then Netflix Drops a Double Surprise After the Bell
raw.summary: Thursday was the second consecutive record close for the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq, and for most of the session the driver was straightforward: TSMC's quarterly numbers were the kind of print that leaves the semiconductor bulls feeling vindicated. Then Netflix reported after the bell, and within minutes the stock was down 8% on two pieces of news the market clearly hadn't priced in, a softer forward outlook and the departure of Reed Hastings from the chairman's seat.
